title: Non-record submission: BigramDim160 + 10% Prune + SWA (1.14767 bpb, 2 seeds)
body:
## Non-record Submission: BigramDim160 + 10% Prune + SWA

**val_bpb: 1.14767** (mean of 2 seeds, post int6+zstd quantization roundtrip, sliding window stride=64)

### Results

| Seed | val_bpb | artifact_bytes | valid |
|------|---------|---------------|-------|
| 42 | 1.14986 | 15,899,011 | yes |
| 1337 | 1.14548 | 15,812,010 | yes |
| **Mean** | **1.14767** | | |
| **Std** | **0.00219** | | |

### Why 2 Seeds

Our third seed (2024) could not be completed due to exhausted compute budget across RunPod and Vast.ai. The two completed seeds show tight variance (std=0.00219). We submit this as a **non-record entry** documenting our approach and are happy to add the third seed when compute is available.

### Key Approach

Builds on the SOTA foundation with artifact-size-focused modifications:

- **BigramHash dim=160** (from 192): Primary knob for reliable sub-16MB artifacts across seeds. Artifact size variance between seeds can exceed 600KB at dim=192.
- **10% weight pruning** (post-SWA): Zeroes smallest 10% of non-embedding linear weights, improving compressibility.
- **SWA, SmearGate, OrthoInit, GQA, Int6+zstd-22**: Carried from SOTA entries.

### Observations

- dim=176 + 10% prune achieved 1.1426 bpb (single run) but produced a 16.69MB artifact -- illustrating the artifact size variance challenge.
- The 16MB limit is the binding constraint, not model quality. Seed-dependent compression variance makes this a reliability problem as much as an optimization one.
- Full training logs were lost when cloud instances were terminated; included logs are excerpts from automation transcripts.

All runs on 8xH100 SXM, 10-minute wallclock, ~6,500 steps, ~91ms/step.
